description: 8 complex system prompts testing compositional Blueprint generation at mid-tier complexity. Each prompt requires 20-35 nodes combining multiple patterns: variable read-modify-write, branching, damage systems, timer loops, custom events, ForLoop, and multi-path logic. Trains the model to sustain correct EXEC chains and DATA wiring across longer sequences.

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with float Health=100, float MaxHealth=100, bool IsDead=false. On AnyDamage: get Health, subtract damage, clamp between 0 and MaxHealth, set Health. Check if Health is less than or equal to 0. If so, set IsDead to true and print Dead. Otherwise print the remaining health.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_CharacterHealth
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0
VAR MaxHealth: Float = 100.0
VAR IsDead: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=MaxHealth]
NODE n5: ClampFloat [Min=0.0]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n7: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n8: Branch
NODE n9: SetVar [Variable=IsDead, Value=true]
NODE n10: PrintString [InString="Dead"]
NODE n11: PrintString
NODE n12: GetVar [Variable=Health]

EXEC n1.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.True -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n8.False -> n11.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Float]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.Max [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Condition [Bool]
DATA n12.Value -> n11.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with int Score=0, bool IsCollected=false. On overlap: get IsCollected, use NotBool to check if not collected, branch on that. If not collected: set IsCollected true, get Score, add 100, set Score, print the score, get actor location, play sound SFX_Pickup at that location, then after a 0.5 second delay destroy self.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PickupEffects
PARENT: Actor

VAR Score: Int = 0
VAR IsCollected: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsCollected]
NODE n3: NotBool
NODE n4: Branch
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=IsCollected, Value=true]
NODE n6: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n7: AddInt [B=100]
NODE n8: SetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n9: PrintString
NODE n10: GetActorLocation
NODE n11: PlaySoundAtLocation [Sound=SFX_Pickup]
NODE n12: Delay [Duration=0.5]
NODE n13: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n12.Execute
EXEC n12.Completed -> n13.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Condition [Bool]
DATA n6.Value -> n7.A [Int]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Value [Int]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n9.InString [String]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n11.Location [Vector]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with float Health=200, float AttackPower=25, bool IsAlive=true. On AnyDamage: get IsAlive, branch. If alive: get Health, subtract the damage amount, set Health. Check if Health is less than or equal to 0. If dead: set IsAlive to false, print Enemy Defeated, then after a 2 second delay destroy actor. If still alive after damage: get Health again and print the remaining health.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_EnemyDamage
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 200.0
VAR AttackPower: Float = 25.0
VAR IsAlive: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsAlive]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n5: SubtractFloat
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n7: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n8: Branch
NODE n9: SetVar [Variable=IsAlive, Value=false]
NODE n10: PrintString [InString="Enemy Defeated"]
NODE n11: Delay [Duration=2.0]
NODE n12: DestroyActor
NODE n13: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n14: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.True -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Completed -> n12.Execute
EXEC n8.False -> n14.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n5.B [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Float]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Condition [Bool]
DATA n13.Value -> n14.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with float DamagePerTick=10, float TickInterval=1.0, bool IsActive=true. On BeginPlay: get IsActive, branch. If active: print Zone Active, then Delay for TickInterval seconds, then call custom event DealDamage. DealDamage: print Damage Dealt, then Delay for TickInterval again, then call DealDamage to loop.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TimedHazard
PARENT: Actor

VAR DamagePerTick: Float = 10.0
VAR TickInterval: Float = 1.0
VAR IsActive: Bool = true

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsActive]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Zone Active"]
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=TickInterval]
NODE n6: Delay
NODE n7: CallFunction [FunctionName="DealDamage"]
NODE n8: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="DealDamage"]
NODE n9: PrintString [InString="Damage Dealt"]
NODE n10: GetVar [Variable=TickInterval]
NODE n11: Delay
NODE n12: CallFunction [FunctionName="DealDamage"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Completed -> n7.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Completed -> n12.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n5.Value -> n6.Duration [Float]
DATA n10.Value -> n11.Duration [Float]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with bool IsLocked=true, bool HasKey=false, int KeysRequired=1, int KeysCollected=0. On overlap: get IsLocked, branch. If locked: get KeysCollected, add 1, set KeysCollected. Get KeysRequired, check if KeysCollected is greater than or equal to KeysRequired. If yes: set IsLocked to false, print Door Unlocked. If no: print Need More Keys. If not locked: print Door Already Open.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DoorLock
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsLocked: Bool = true
VAR HasKey: Bool = false
VAR KeysRequired: Int = 1
VAR KeysCollected: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsLocked]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=KeysCollected]
NODE n5: AddInt [B=1]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=KeysCollected]
NODE n7: GetVar [Variable=KeysRequired]
NODE n8: GreaterThanOrEqual
NODE n9: Branch
NODE n10: SetVar [Variable=IsLocked, Value=false]
NODE n11: PrintString [InString="Door Unlocked"]
NODE n12: PrintString [InString="Need More Keys"]
NODE n13: PrintString [InString="Door Already Open"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.True -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n9.False -> n12.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n13.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]
DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n8.A [Int]
DATA n7.Value -> n8.B [Int]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with int TimeRemaining=30, bool TimerRunning=false. On BeginPlay: set TimerRunning to true, then call custom event TickTimer. TickTimer: get TimeRemaining, check if greater than 0. If yes: subtract 1, set TimeRemaining, print the time, Delay 1.0 second, then call TickTimer again to loop. If no: set TimerRunning to false, print Time Up.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Countdown
PARENT: Actor

VAR TimeRemaining: Int = 30
VAR TimerRunning: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetVar [Variable=TimerRunning, Value=true]
NODE n3: CallFunction [FunctionName="TickTimer"]
NODE n4: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="TickTimer"]
NODE n5: GetVar [Variable=TimeRemaining]
NODE n6: GreaterThan [B=0]
NODE n7: Branch
NODE n8: SubtractInt [B=1]
NODE n9: SetVar [Variable=TimeRemaining]
NODE n10: PrintString
NODE n11: Delay [Duration=1.0]
NODE n12: CallFunction [FunctionName="TickTimer"]
NODE n13: SetVar [Variable=TimerRunning, Value=false]
NODE n14: PrintString [InString="Time Up"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.True -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Completed -> n12.Execute
EXEC n7.False -> n13.Execute
EXEC n13.Then -> n14.Execute

DATA n5.Value -> n6.A [Int]
DATA n6.ReturnValue -> n7.Condition [Bool]
DATA n5.Value -> n8.A [Int]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.Value [Int]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n10.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with int CurrentWave=0, int MaxWaves=3, int EnemiesPerWave=5, bool IsSpawning=false. On BeginPlay: call custom event StartWave. StartWave: get CurrentWave, add 1, set CurrentWave. Get MaxWaves, check if CurrentWave is less than or equal to MaxWaves. If yes: set IsSpawning true, print Wave Starting, use ForLoop from 0 to EnemiesPerWave minus 1 to print Spawning Enemy in the loop body. After the loop completes, print Wave Complete, Delay 3.0 seconds, then call StartWave again. If no: print All Waves Complete.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_WaveSpawner
PARENT: Actor

VAR CurrentWave: Int = 0
VAR MaxWaves: Int = 3
VAR EnemiesPerWave: Int = 5
VAR IsSpawning: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: CallFunction [FunctionName="StartWave"]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="StartWave"]
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=CurrentWave]
NODE n5: AddInt [B=1]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=CurrentWave]
NODE n7: GetVar [Variable=MaxWaves]
NODE n8: LessThanOrEqual
NODE n9: Branch
NODE n10: SetVar [Variable=IsSpawning, Value=true]
NODE n11: PrintString [InString="Wave Starting"]
NODE n12: GetVar [Variable=EnemiesPerWave]
NODE n13: SubtractInt [B=1]
NODE n14: ForLoop [FirstIndex=0]
NODE n15: PrintString [InString="Spawning Enemy"]
NODE n16: PrintString [InString="Wave Complete"]
NODE n17: Delay [Duration=3.0]
NODE n18: CallFunction [FunctionName="StartWave"]
NODE n19: PrintString [InString="All Waves Complete"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.True -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n14.Execute
EXEC n14.LoopBody -> n15.Execute
EXEC n14.Completed -> n16.Execute
EXEC n16.Then -> n17.Execute
EXEC n17.Completed -> n18.Execute
EXEC n9.False -> n19.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n8.A [Int]
DATA n7.Value -> n8.B [Int]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.Condition [Bool]
DATA n12.Value -> n13.A [Int]
DATA n13.ReturnValue -> n14.LastIndex [Int]

prompts.instruction: Create a Blueprint with bool IsOn=false. On overlap: get IsOn, flip it with NotBool, set IsOn to the flipped value. Branch on the new value. If on: print Switch Activated, set actor scale to 1.5 using MakeVector and SetActorScale3D, play sound SFX_Activate at actor location. If off: print Switch Deactivated, set actor scale to 1.0 using MakeVector and SetActorScale3D.
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_InteractiveSwitch
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsOn: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsOn]
NODE n3: NotBool
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=IsOn]
NODE n5: Branch
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Switch Activated"]
NODE n7: MakeVector [X=1.5, Y=1.5, Z=1.5]
NODE n8: SetActorScale3D
NODE n9: GetActorLocation
NODE n10: PlaySoundAtLocation [Sound=SFX_Activate]
NODE n11: PrintString [InString="Switch Deactivated"]
NODE n12: MakeVector [X=1.0, Y=1.0, Z=1.0]
NODE n13: SetActorScale3D

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.True -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n5.False -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n13.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.Condition [Bool]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.NewScale3D [Vector]
DATA n9.ReturnValue -> n10.Location [Vector]
DATA n12.ReturnValue -> n13.NewScale3D [Vector]
